description: Add POST /recipes/import endpoint to the Lambda handler that: (1) receives a URL in the request body, (2) fetches the page content from that URL, (3) sends it to AWS Bedrock (model eu.amazon.nova-lite-v1:0) with a prompt containing 2-3 example recipes from the data-model.md steering file to convert the content to CreateRecipeInput format, (4) saves the recipe to DynamoDB, and (5) returns the created recipe. Also add @aws-sdk/client-bedrock-runtime to package.json dependencies.
steps: Add @aws-sdk/client-bedrock-runtime to the root package.json dependencies and run pnpm install

In apps/api/src/handler.ts, add a new route match for POST /recipes/import (before the /recipes/{id} match so it doesn't get captured by the regex). The route handler should: extract userId from authorizer claims, parse body to get the url field, validate it.

Implement a function that fetches the web page content from the given URL using Node.js built-in fetch (available in Node 24). Strip HTML tags to extract text content for the Bedrock prompt. Keep only meaningful text (strip scripts, styles, nav elements).

Implement the Bedrock invocation using BedrockRuntimeClient and InvokeModelCommand from @aws-sdk/client-bedrock-runtime. Use model ID 'eu.amazon.nova-lite-v1:0'. The prompt must include 2-3 of the example recipes from the data-model.md steering file (Bienenstich, One-Pot Pasta, Baby Pizza) in CreateRecipeInput format (without id, userId, createdAt, updatedAt) as few-shot examples. The prompt should instruct the model to extract the recipe from the page content and return a single JSON object matching the CreateRecipeInput structure.
Parse the Bedrock response JSON into CreateRecipeInput format. Add error handling for malformed responses.
Save the parsed recipe to DynamoDB (same as createRecipe logic - generate id, set userId, add timestamps) and return the created recipe with 201 status.
Add BEDROCK_REGION environment variable support (default to process.env['AWS_REGION'] or 'eu-central-1') for the BedrockRuntimeClient configuration.
acceptance_criteria: POST /recipes/import route is handled in handler.ts before /recipes/{id} route matching
Request body with { url: string } is validated - returns 400 if url is missing
Page content is fetched from the provided URL
Bedrock is called with model eu.amazon.nova-lite-v1:0 and a prompt containing 2-3 example recipes in CreateRecipeInput format
Bedrock response is parsed as JSON into CreateRecipeInput structure
Recipe is saved to DynamoDB with generated id, userId, and timestamps
Response is 201 with the created recipe
Error cases return appropriate status codes (400 for bad input, 502 for Bedrock/fetch failures)
pnpm nx build api succeeds without type errors
verification: Run pnpm nx build api and confirm it compiles without errors
